channel.title: Dancebreak – Welcome to Dancebreak!
channel.description: Recent content in Welcome to Dancebreak! on Dancebreak

item.title: Spring 2026
item.description:
Dancebreak will be in Roble 113, 7:00-10:00 PM on Mondays this spring!

Our beginner workshops (bolded) and dance socials are open to everyone! Intermediate workshops assume some social dance experience.

Time Location Description Category
3/30
7:00 PM–8:00 PM 		Roble 114 Weird Waltzing (Akshay Srivatsan and Ness) Intermediate Workshop
3/30
7:00 PM–8:00 PM 		Roble 113 Beginner Rotary Waltz (Akshay Smit + Meera) Beginner Workshop

8:00 PM–10:00 PM 		Roble 113 Social Dance (Samuel and Akshay Srivatsan) Social Dance

There are also many other local dance events, pretty much every day of the week!

item.title: Austria Fortnight (Winter 2026)
item.description:
Get ready for the Stanford Viennese Ball with Stanford’s fortnight of beginner dance lessons!

Every year, the Stanford Viennese Ball committee runs a series of daily workshops on various dance styles. Members of various Stanford social dance clubs collaborate to teach beginner and intermediate lessons in the many partner dance styles done at Stanford.

Austria Fortnight 2026 will start on February 13th (with the Bon Bon Ball) and end on February 27st (with the Viennese Ball).

For up to date info, see the Austria Fortnight Website.

Last updated: February 6, 2026

item.title: Winter 2026
item.description:
Dancebreak will be in Roble 113, 8:30-11:30 PM on Mondays this winter!

Our beginner workshops (bolded) and dance socials are open to everyone! Intermediate workshops assume some social dance experience.

Date Time Location Description Category
1/5 8:30 PM → 9:30 PM Roble 113 Cross-Step Waltz (Akshay Srivatsan & Bella) Beginner Workshop

There are also many other local dance events, pretty much every day of the week!

item.description:

item.title: Summer 2025
item.description:
Dancebreak continues through the summer! We will be in Koret Pavilion, with workshops from 7-8 and social dancing from 8-11.

Dancebreak

Our beginner workshops and dance socials are open to everyone! Intermediate and advanced workshops assume some social dance experience.

Week Date Location Time Description
1 9/22 Roble 113 7:00-8:00 Beginner Workshop: Cross-Step Waltz with Sophia and Steven
Roble 113 8:00-10:00 Social Dancing DJed by Briana
2 9/29 Roble 113 7:00-8:00 Beginner Workshop: West Coast Swing with Bella and Hikari
Roble 113 8:00-10:00 Social Dancing DJed by Bella and Hikari
3 10/06 Roble 113 7:00-8:00 Beginner Workshop: Cross-Step Waltz
Roble 113 8:00-10:00 Social Dancing DJed by Akshay Srivatsan

Last updated: September 25, 2025

There are also many other local dance events, pretty much every day of the week!

item.title: All That Jazz (Spring 2025, SSD Crossover)
item.description:
Join us for a Dancebreak/Stanford Swing Dancers crossover event!

This Saturday April 19th, Stanford Swing Dancers and Dancebreak Present: All That Jazz!

Join us for a Lindy Hop and waltz crossover event with workshops and social dancing! Bring your friends, lovers, loose acquaintances, and enemies to a fantastic night — flier below!

5-7 pm in RAG 116: Beginner crossover Lindy hop and waltz workshop! If you have danced neither dance before or know only a little bit of one or the other (e.g. not meeting intermediate qualifications for one of the two styles), this is for you — we’ll give you a crash course in Lindy hop and in the social waltzes done here at Stanford!

5-7pm in Roble 114: Intermediate crossover Lindy hop and waltz workshop! If you are meeting the criteria below for at least one of the two dance styles, join us for an intermediate crossover workshop focused on using what you already know in one style to get a jumpstart in the other style, and to make each style feel more comfortable!

Lindy hop prerequisites: comfortable with 6 and 8 count footwork, tuck turns, swingouts and Lindy circles, groove walks/lollies

Waltz prerequisites: rotary and/or cross step waltz basic steps, polka basic steps, turns, waltz walks, pancakes

7-10pm in Roble 113: Social dancing!

Three spectacular hour-long sets:

  1. All That Jazz (Lindy Hop to classic Lindy music + jazz waltzes),
  2. None Of That Jazz (Lindy to ostensibly-Lindy-able music that’s not necessarily your typical SSD fare + fave waltzes from Dancebreak), and the
  3. Last Set (to be determined based on which group is better represented, with more Lindy if there are more Lindy folks and more waltz if there are more waltz folks)

item.title: Events (Spring 2025)
item.description:
This quarter we’ll mostly be in Roble 113 from 9:30-11:30 PM, but this may change on a week-by-week basis depending on room availability.

Dancebreak

We usually have a one-hour lesson before each social.

Week Date Location Time Theme
1 3/31 EPC 8-10 April Fools
2 4/7 EPC 8-10
3 4/14 EPC 8-10
4/19 Roble 113 7-9 All That Jazz with SSD
4 4/21 Roble 113 9:30-11:30

6 5/5 Roble 113 9:30-11:30
7 5/12 Roble 113 9:30-11:30
8 5/19 Roble 113 10:30-11:30
9 5/26 Roble 113 9:30-11:30
10 6/2 EPC 9:00-11:00
6/9 GCC Havana 8:00-10:00

Last updated: June 11, 2025

Other Socials

These events aren’t run by Dancebreak, but may be of interest. If you have other on-campus social dance events you want us to list here, let us know!

Date Name Organizers Dance Styles Location Time

5/3 Floorcraft Timmie Wong Various GCC Havana 7-11 PM
5/9 Big Dance Gang of 13 Various Roble 113 9 PM-6 AM
5/30 Jammix Richard Powers Various Roble 113 9 PM-12 AM

There are also many other local dance events, pretty much every day of the week!

item.title: Workshops (Spring 2025)
item.description:
Learn to dance or pick up new skills with our free workshops!

All Workshops

Workshops are followed by a two-hour social, either in the same room or nearby.

Week Date Topic Level Instructors Location Time
1 3/31 Waltzing Wrong Intermediate Bella and Akshay1 EPC 7-8
2 4/7 Charleston Beginner Wally and Dakota EPC 7-8
3 4/14 Rotary Waltz Beginner Lyna and Gokul EPC 7-8
4/19 Rotary Waltz and Lindy Hop Beginner Ariel and Akshay1 Roble 116 5-7
4/19 Rotary Waltz and Lindy Hop Intermediate Maiya and Annika Roble 114 5-7
4 4/21 Dawn Mazurka Review2 Advanced Bandana Bob Roble 116 8-9:30
5 4/28 Dawn Mazurka Review2 Advanced Bandana Bob Roble 116 8-9:30
6 5/5 Cross-Step Waltz Beginner Bella and Akshay1 Roble 116 8-9:30
5/9 Cross-Step Waltz (at Big Dance) Beginner Sophia and Akshay1 Roble 115 8-9
5/9 Cross-Step Waltz (at Big Dance) Intermediate Shelly and Jason Roble 115 8-9
7 5/12 Waltz Intermediate Gokul and Bella Roble 116 8-9
5/12 Waltz Beginner Gokul and Bella Roble 116 9-9:30
8 5/19 Nightclub Two Step Beginner Eddy and Jasmin Roble 113 9:30-10:30
9 5/26 Reverse Waltz Intermediate Akshay and Akshay Roble 114 7:30-8:30
5/26 Cross-Step Waltz Beginner Ashley and Akshay3 Roble 114 8:30-9:30
10 6/2 How to DJ Intermediate Richard Powers EPC 7:00-7:45
6/2 Body Rolls Beginner Chhavi EPC 7:45-8:00
6/2 Fusion Beginner Chhavi EPC 8:00-9:00
6/9 Hustle Beginner Ashley and Akshay1 GCC Havana 7:00-8:00

Last updated: June 11, 2025

For more lessons, check out Friday Night Waltz, Stanford Swing Dances, or Cardinal West Coast Swing.

  1. Akshay Srivatsan ↩︎ ↩︎ ↩︎ ↩︎ ↩︎

  2. This class requires prior knowledge of the Dawn Mazurka choreography; we will not teach the choreography from scratch due to time constraints. ↩︎ ↩︎

  3. Akshay Smit ↩︎

item.title: Austria Fortnight (Winter 2025)
item.description:
Get ready for the Stanford Viennese Ball with Stanford’s fortnight of beginner dance lessons!

📆
Getting ready for the 2026 Viennese Ball? Click here to see the schedule for Austria Fortnight 2026!

Every year, the Stanford Viennese Ball committee run a series of daily workshops on various dance styles. Members of various Stanford social dance clubs—Dancebreak, DanceSport, Stanford Viennese Ball, Swingtime, Stanford Swing Dancers, Cardinal West Coast Swing, Stanford Tango, and more—all collaborate to teach beginner and intermediate lessons in the many partner dance styles done at Stanford.

Austria Fortnight 2025 will start on February 7th (with Bon Bon Ball) and end on February 21st (with Viennese Ball).

Check out the workshop schedule here!

Last updated: February 4, 2026

item.title: Events (Winter 2025)
item.description:
This quarter we’ll mostly be in Roble 113 from 7-9 PM, but this may change on a week-by-week basis depending on room availability.

Dancebreak

We usually have a one-hour lesson before each social.

Week Date DJ Location Time Theme

2 1/13 Sophia Roble 113 7-9
3 1/20 Akshay1 EPC 8-10

10 3/10 Ethan and Dakota EPC 8-10
FW 3/17 Eddy GCC Havana 8-10 St. Patrick’s Day
SB 3/24 Andrea EVGR C153 8-10

Last updated: April 21, 2025

Viennese Ball

The biggest Stanford ball of the year is the Stanford Viennese Ball. This year, Dancebreak is providing a limited number of financial aid tickets for members who would otherwise be unable to attend the Viennese Ball for financial reasons; fill out this form to be considered for financial aid tickets. Priority will go to applicants with financial need and Dancebreak regulars.

Other Socials

These events aren’t run by Dancebreak, but may be of interest. If you have other on-campus social dance events you want us to list here, let us know!

Date Name Organizers Dance Styles Location Time
1/18 SSD Social Stanford Swing Dancers Lindy
1/24 Jammix Richard Powers Various Roble 113 9 PM-12 AM
2/7 Bon Bon Ball Richard Powers and Stanford Viennese Ball Various Roble 113 7 PM-12 AM
2/21 Viennese Ball Stanford Viennese Ball Various Marriot Marquis San Francisco 6 PM-2 AM
3/14 Jammix Richard Powers Various Roble 113 9 PM-12 AM

  1. Akshay Smit ↩︎ ↩︎

  2. Akshay Srivatsan ↩︎

item.title: Workshops (Winter 2025)
item.description:
Learn to dance or pick up new skills with our free workshops!

All Workshops

Workshops are followed by a two-hour social, either in the same room or nearby.

Week Date Topic Instructors Location Time
1 1/6 Cross-Step Waltz Grace and Will GCC Havana 7-8

3 1/20 West Coast Swing Akshay1 and Jiying EPC 7-8

Last updated: April 21, 2025

For more lessons, check out Friday Night Waltz, Stanford Swing Dances, or Cardinal West Coast Swing.

Austria Fortnight

In the fortnight leading up to Stanford Viennese Ball (Febuary 7–21), Stanford dancers traditionally teach workshops every night in many different styles of dance. Check out the Austria Fortnight schedule here.

  1. Akshay Smit ↩︎

  2. Akshay Srivatsan ↩︎ ↩︎

item.title: Events (Autumn 2024)
item.description:
This quarter we’ll mostly be in Roble 113 from 7-9 PM, but this may change on a week-by-week basis depending on room availability.

Last updated: January 1, 2025

Dancebreak

In general, three hour timeslots indicate a lesson/workshop in the first hour.

Week Date DJ Location Time Theme
1 9/23 Jason EVGR-B140 7-11
2 9/30 Sophia and Akshay1 Roble 113 7-9 Broadway
3 10/7 Audrey GCC Havana 7-10
4 10/14 Saumya Roble 113 7-9 Diwali
5 10/21 Akshay2 Elliott Program Center 7-10
6 10/28 Briana Elliott Program Center 7-10
7 11/4 Wally GCC Havana 7-10 Video Games
8 11/11 Cordelia Roble 113 7-10
9 11/18 Maiya Roble 113 7-9
🦃 11/25 Akshay1 Roble 113 7-9 Food
10 12/2 Akshay2 EVGR-C 7-9:30
✍ 12/9 Eddy Elliott Program Center 7-9 Country Music + Holidays

Information in italics is still tentative. If you’re interested in DJing one of the open spots, let us know!

Other Socials

These events aren’t run by Dancebreak, but may be of interest. If you have other on-campus social dance events you want us to list here, let us know!

Date Name Organizers Dance Styles Location Time
10/5 Sugar High Stanford Swing Dancers Lindy EPC 7-11 PM
10/18 Jammix Richard Powers Various Roble 113 9 PM-12 AM
11/2 East Meets West SSD and Cardinal Swing Lindy and WCS Roble 113 7-10 PM
11/8 Fall Ball Richard Powers Various Roble 113 8:30 PM-12 AM
12/6 Jammix Richard Powers Various Roble 113 9 PM-12 AM

Fall Ball

Stanford Fall Ball 2024: Egyptian Masquerade🏺🌙

Join us for an unforgettable night of glamour and intrigue! 🎭✨

  • 📆 Nov 8, 8:30pm - 12:00am
  • 📍 Roble Gym Room 113
  • 🎫 $5 for Stanford students | $15 for general public

Dancebreak is providing free Fall Ball tickets for up to 50 Dancebreak members: sign up here by 10/30 to be considered. If you signed up by 10/30, you should have received tickets!

Featuring:

  • Cross-Step Waltz lesson at 7:30pm
  • music DJed by Richard Powers
  • performances by Swingtime and Dance Libre
  • food and refreshments

More Info and Tickets: Eventbrite

Auditions

These performance groups do styles of dance we often do at Dancebreak.

Date Group Dance Styles Location Time
9/28 Viennese Ball Waltz and Polka Roble 113 10 AM-12 PM
9/28 Swingtime Lindy Hop Roble 113 2-5 PM
10/26 Cardinal Swing (register here) West Coast Swing EVGR-C 2:30-4:30 PM

  1. Akshay Srivatsan ↩︎ ↩︎

  2. Akshay Smit ↩︎ ↩︎

item.title: Workshops (Autumn 2024)
item.description:
We have several workshops planned this quarter, for beginners and experienced dancers alike! If you’re new, join us for our beginner waltz sequence and waltz office hours in October or any of the other beginner lessons throughout the quarter.

Last updated: January 1, 2025

All Workshops

Week Date Topic Instructors Location Time
1 9/25 Viennese Waltz Emily and Angelina Rains Hacienda 6-9
1 9/26 Polka Josh and Bella GCC Havana 6-9
3 10/7 Beginner Rotary Waltz Akshay1 and Ashley GCC Havana 7-8
4 10/14 Office Hours Roble 113 7-8
5 10/21 Beginner Cross-Step Waltz Shelly and Akshay2 Elliott Program Center 7-8
6 10/28 Intermediate Cross-Step Waltz Cameron and Rocky Elliott Program Center 7-8
7 11/4 Beginner Rotary Waltz Sophia and Jason GCC Havana 7-8
8 11/11 Beginner+ Rotary Waltz Gokul and Bella Roble 113 7-8
9 11/18 Mystery Lindy (beginner-friendly) Maiya and Max Roble 116 5:30-6:30

Information in italics is still tentative.

If you’re looking for even more, our friends at Stanford Swing Dancers and Cardinal West Coast Swing teach workshops in Lindy Hop and West Coast Swing almost every week on campus. Friday Night Waltz also runs weekly lessons on various kinds of dance just off-campus.

Descriptions

Viennese Ball Audition Workshops (Week 1)

We partner with the Viennese Ball Opening Committee to run workshops before their auditions.

Auditions are 9/28 at 10:00 AM in Roble 113.

Beginner Waltz Sequence (Weeks 3-8)

Come learn to Waltz with us!

  • Rotary Waltz is the traditional style of waltz, done since the late 18th century. We do it to a wide range of pop songs.
  • Cross-Step Waltz is a more modern form of waltz which is really popular at Stanford (and invented here in the 90s, at least in part).

Our beginner workshops assume no dance experience of any sort (although experienced dancers are welcome as well, of course). Our “office hours” are an informal way to learn to dance, to practice/get feedback, or to hone a particular skill.

  1. Akshay Srivatsan ↩︎

  2. Akshay Smit ↩︎
